[35m❯[0m cat wikipedia.txt 
[?2004lThe white stork (Ciconia ciconia) is a large bird in the stork family, Ciconiidae. Its plumage is mainly white, with black on its wings. Adults have long red legs and long pointed red beaks, and measure on average 100 to 115 centimetres (39 to 45 inches) from the beak tip to the end of the tail, with a wingspan of 155 to 215 centimetres (61 to 85 inches). It is a long-distance migrant, wintering in Africa from tropical sub-Saharan Africa to as far south as South Africa, or on the Indian subcontinent. A carnivore, it eats a wide range of animal prey, including insects, fish, amphibians, reptiles, small mammals, and small birds. This white stork in flight was photographed in Andalusia, Spain, carrying a piece of plastic. It is fitted with a wildlife transmitter on its back.
[?2004h

[?2004lThe white stork (Ciconia ciconia) is a large bird in the stork family, 
Ciconiidae. Its plumage is mainly white, with black on its wings. Adults have 
long red legs and long pointed red beaks, and measure on average 100 to 115 
centimetres (39 to 45 inches) from the beak tip to the end of the tail, with a 
wingspan of 155 to 215 centimetres (61 to 85 inches). It is a long-distance 
migrant, wintering in Africa from tropical sub-Saharan Africa to as far south 
as South Africa, or on the Indian subcontinent. A carnivore, it eats a wide 
range of animal prey, including insects, fish, amphibians, reptiles, small 
mammals, and small birds. This white stork in flight was photographed in 
Andalusia, Spain, carrying a piece of plastic. It is fitted with a wildlife 
transmitter on its back.

[H[2J[3JThe white stork (Ciconia ciconia) is a large bird in the stork family, 
Ciconiidae. Its plumage is mainly white, with black on its wings. Adults have 
long red legs and long pointed red beaks, and measure on average 100 to 115 
centimetres (39 to 45 inches) from the beak tip to the end of the tail, with a 
wingspan of 155 to 215 centimetres (61 to 85 inches). It is a long-distance 
migrant, wintering in Africa from tropical sub-Saharan Africa to as far south 
as South Africa, or on the Indian subcontinent. A carnivore, it eats a wide 
range of animal prey, including insects, fish, amphibians, reptiles, small 
mammals, and small birds. This white stork in flight was photographed in 
Andalusia, Spain, carrying a piece of plastic. It is fitted with a wildlife 
transmitter on its back.

range of animal prey, including insects, fish, amphibians, reptiles, small 
mammals, and small birds. This white stork in flight was photographed in 
Andalusia, Spain, carrying a piece of plastic. It is fitted with a wildlife 
transmitter on its back.
[H[2J[3JThe white stork (Ciconia ciconia) is a large bird in the stork family, 
Ciconiidae. Its plumage is mainly white, with black on its wings. Adults have 
long red legs and long pointed red beaks, and measure on average 100 to 115 
centimetres (39 to 45 inches) from the beak tip to the end of the tail, with a 
wingspan of 155 to 215 centimetres (61 to 85 inches). It is a long-distance 
migrant, wintering in Africa from tropical sub-Saharan Africa to as far south 
as South Africa, or on the Indian subcontinent. A carnivore, it eats a wide 
range of animal prey, including insects, fish, amphibians, reptiles, small 
mammals, and small birds. This white stork in flight was photographed in 
Andalusia, Spain, carrying a piece of plastic. It is fitted with a wildlife 
transmitter on its back.

as South Africa, or on[01;31m[K the [m[KIndian subcontinent. A carnivore, it eats a wide

Ciconiidae.[01;31m[K Its [m[Kplumage is mainly white, with black on[01;31m[K its [m[Kwings. Adults have 
long[01;31m[K red [m[Klegs[01;31m[K and [m[Klong pointed[01;31m[K red [m[Kbeaks,[01;31m[K and [m[Kmeasure on average[01;31m[K 100 [m[Kto[01;31m[K 115 [m[K

migrant, wintering in Africa from tropical sub-Saharan Africa to as[01;31m[K far [m[Ksouth 
as South Africa, or on[01;31m[K the [m[KIndian subcontinent. A carnivore, it eats a wide

long[01;31m[K red [m[Klegs[01;31m[K and [m[Klong pointed[01;31m[K red [m[Kbeaks,[01;31m[K and [m[Kmeasure on average[01;31m[K 100 [m[Kto[01;31m[K 115 [m[K
centimetres[01;31m[K (39 [m[Kto 45 inches) from[01;31m[K the [m[Kbeak[01;31m[K tip [m[Kto[01;31m[K the [m[Kend of[01;31m[K the [m[Ktail, with a 
wingspan of[01;31m[K 155 [m[Kto[01;31m[K 215 [m[Kcentimetres[01;31m[K (61 [m[Kto 85 inches). It is a long-distance 
migrant, wintering in Africa from tropical sub-Saharan Africa to as[01;31m[K far [m[Ksouth 
as South Africa, or on[01;31m[K the [m[KIndian subcontinent. A carnivore, it eats a wide 
mammals,[01;31m[K and [m[Ksmall birds. This white stork in flight[01;31m[K was [m[Kphotographed in 
transmitter on[01;31m[K its [m[Kback.

Ciconiidae.[01;31m[K Its [m[Kplumage is mainly white, with black on[01;31m[K its [m[Kwings. Adults have

migrant, wintering in Africa from tropical sub-Saharan Africa to as[01;31m[K far [m[Ksouth

Ciconiidae.[01;31m[K Its [m[Kplumage is mainly white, with black on[01;31m[K its [m[Kwings. Adults have 
long[01;31m[K red [m[Klegs[01;31m[K and [m[Klong pointed[01;31m[K red [m[Kbeaks,[01;31m[K and [m[Kmeasure on average[01;31m[K 100 [m[Kto[01;31m[K 115 [m[K
centimetres[01;31m[K (39 [m[Kto 45 inches) from[01;31m[K the [m[Kbeak[01;31m[K tip [m[Kto[01;31m[K the [m[Kend of[01;31m[K the [m[Ktail, with a 
wingspan of[01;31m[K 155 [m[Kto[01;31m[K 215 [m[Kcentimetres[01;31m[K (61 [m[Kto 85 inches). It is a long-distance 
migrant, wintering in Africa from tropical sub-Saharan Africa to as[01;31m[K far [m[Ksouth 
as South Africa, or on[01;31m[K the [m[KIndian subcontinent. A carnivore, it eats a wide

migrant, wintering in Africa from tropical sub-Saharan Africa to as[01;31m[K far [m[Ksouth 
as South Africa, or on[01;31m[K the [m[KIndian subcontinent. A carnivore, it eats a wide
